preamble: The diphthong /ou/, typically a back rounded vowel glide, can undergo a notable phonetic transformation to /ai/, a front unrounded vowel glide. This shift involves a significant change in the articulatory position, moving the diphthong's trajectory from a back, rounded configuration to a front, unrounded one.
phoneticEffects: Fronting of the diphthong's onset and offset, accompanied by a loss of lip rounding (unrounding)

examples.note: A shift observed in the formation of converbs from plain forms.

sources: Franklin, K. J. (2006). A Comparative Dictionary of Orokolo, Gulf of Papua. Pacific Linguistics.
